remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with hyperacidity because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity. It contains antispasmodic properties that help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, which can prevent acid reflux and reduce the production of stomach acid. Additionally, chamomile tea is known for its calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of hyperacidity. Furthermore, it has been shown to have a protective effect on the lining of the stomach, preventing damage caused by excessive stomach acid. Lastly, chamomile tea is a natural alternative to over-the-counter medications for hyperacidity, which can have side effects and long-term health risks, making it a safer and more gentle option for managing this condition.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The chamomile flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce symptoms of hyperacidity.
Leaves: Chamomile leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, contributing to its calming effects, which may help alleviate acid reflux.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain essential oils, which can aid in digestion and help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, beneficial for treating hyperacidity.
remedy_constituents: Azulene: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a symptom of hyperacidity.
Bisabolol: Possesses anti-spasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to hyperacidity.
Chamazulene: Exhibits strong anti-oxidant properties that can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by acid reflux.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and damage in the digestive tract.
Matricin: This compound is converted to chamazulene in the steam distillation process and has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that can help reduce symptoms of hyperacidity.
Apigenin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a teapot or kettle for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in one teaspoon of honey (optional) for added soothing properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, allowing the chamomile flowers to remain in the infuser or teapot. Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature and enjoy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which can increase the mucus lining of the stomach, protecting it from excessive acid. This action can help prevent the irritation and inflammation caused by hyperacidity. Moreover, licorice tea has been shown to inhibit the production of gastric acid, providing relief from symptoms of acid reflux. However, it's important to note that excessive consumption of licorice root can lead to side effects like high blood pressure, so it's recommended to consume licorice tea in moderation or opt for deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) tea, which has had the glycyrrhizin compound removed. Lastly, while licorice tea can be a helpful supplement, it should not replace any medication prescribed by a healthcare professional for managing hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to their ability to balance acid production in the stomach.
Stems: The stems of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizic acid, which helps soothe stomach lining and reduce inflammation caused by excessive acidity.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and indigestion.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which can help regulate stomach acid production and provide relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain antioxidants that can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by hyperacidity and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin is the main active component of licorice, and has been shown to inhibit gastric acid secretion, making it effective for managing hyperacidity.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains various flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin, which have been found to protect gastric mucosa and reduce gastric acid secretion, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
Coumarins: Licorice contains coumarin derivatives, like glabridin, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help reduce gastric inflammation caused by hyperacidity.
Volatile oils: The volatile oils in licorice, including anethole and linalool, have carminative properties, helping to relieve symptoms of hyperacidity such as bloating and gas.
Chalcones: Licorice contains chalcones, such as licochalcone A, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a major cause of hyperacidity and gastritis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root and reduce heat, simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off heat, let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root, then let it cool down.
Drink 1 cup of licorice tea, up to three times a day, before or after meals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains mucilage, a substance that can coat and protect the lining of the stomach, reducing irritation and inflammation. This soothing effect can help to alleviate the symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn, stomach pain, and acid reflux. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can further reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing. Furthermore, the tea has been shown to have a demulcent effect, which means it can form a protective film over the mucous membranes, providing additional relief from acid reflux and related discomforts. Finally, marshmallow tea is a natural remedy, free from the side effects that often accompany conventional medications for hyperacidity, making it a gentle and safe option for those looking to manage their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant are used for medicinal tea for hyperacidity due to their mucilage content, which can help protect the stomach lining.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers are used in tea for hyperacidity because of their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the marshmallow plant are used in medicinal tea for hyperacidity as they contain large amounts of mucilage that can help balance gastric juices.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are used in medicinal tea for hyperacidity because they contain high levels of mucilage, which can help protect the stomach from excess acid.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a high amount of mucilage, which helps to form a protective layer on the mucous membranes of the stomach, reducing irritation and hyperacidity.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates, found in marshmallow, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing the symptoms of hyperacidity.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in marshmallow have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid, thereby reducing hyperacidity.
Pectin: Marshmallow contains pectin, a type of soluble fiber that can help regulate stomach acid production, providing relief from the symptoms of hyperacidity.
Tannins: The tannins in marshmallow have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, thereby reducing hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Infuse a teaspoon of chamomile flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes to soothe the stomach.
Stir in a tablespoon of honey to help coat the stomach and reduce acidity.
Add a pinch of baking soda to help neutralize stomach acid.
Slowly mix in 3-4 melted marshmallows to thicken the tea and provide a protective barrier for the esophagus.
Steep for an additional 2-3 minutes, then strain and sip slowly to alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and gel-like when mixed with water. This gel-like substance can create a protective barrier in the gastrointestinal tract, helping to prevent acid from backing up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. Additionally, slippery elm tea is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. The tea may also help to stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, which can provide further protection against acid. Furthermore, slippery elm tea is a natural remedy that is generally considered safe and does not have the same risks or side effects as over-the-counter or prescription acid-reducing medications. However, it is important to note that while slippery elm tea may help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, it is not a substitute for medical treatment and individuals experiencing persistent or severe symptoms should consult a healthcare provider.
remedy_parts: Bark: The inner bark of the slippery elm tree is commonly used for making medicinal tea for hyperacidity due to its high mucilage content, which helps coat and soothe the digestive tract.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are used for their demulcent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gastrointestinal tract, thus alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
Seeds: The seeds of slippery elm contain substantial amounts of mucilage, which can help protect the stomach lining and aid in the reduction of acid reflux associated with hyperacidity.
Roots: Although less commonly used than the bark, leaves, and seeds, slippery elm roots also contain mucilage, contributing to the plant's demulcent properties, beneficial in treating hyperacidity.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: Slippery elm's demulcent mucilage forms a slick, protective gel when mixed with water, which can help soothe and protect the digestive lining from acidity and irritation.
Tannins: The astringent tannins in slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
Starch: Slippery elm contains a significant amount of starch, which can act as a buffer, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and providing relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Flavonoids: Slippery elm's flavonoids contribute to its gastroprotective effects, fighting free radicals, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting the growth of acid-producing bacteria in the stomach.
Sterols: Slippery elm's sterols can help decrease inflammation and protect the gastric mucosa, helping to minimize symptoms associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: "In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried slippery elm bark."
"Bring the mixture to a boil, then lower heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes."
"Remove from heat, then strain the tea to remove the bark pieces."
"Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature."

remedies.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating the symptoms of hyperacidity. Additionally, meadowsweet tea has been shown to stimulate the production of prostaglandins, which protect the stomach lining and promote healing. The tea also helps to balance stomach acid levels, reducing the painful symptoms of acid reflux. Furthermore, meadowsweet tea has astringent properties, which help to tighten and heal damaged tissues in the stomach and esophagus, providing relief from the discomfort of hyperacidity. Finally, meadowsweet tea is a natural alternative to over-the-counter antacids, which can have negative side effects when used frequently, making it a safer and more gentle option for long-term use.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Meadowsweet flowers contain salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and acidity in the stomach, making it an ideal part for medicinal tea.
Leaves: The leaves of meadowsweet are rich in tannins, which can help reduce stomach acid and soothe gastrointestinal irritation, providing relief for hyperacidity.
Stems: Meadowsweet stems also contain tannins and flavonoids, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production, making it useful for medicinal tea.

remedy_constituents: Salicylic Acid: Meadowsweet contains salicylic acid, which is a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound. It can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby promoting healing and preventing further damage.

Tannins: Meadowsweet is rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help protect and soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract. By reducing inflammation, tannins can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

Flavonoids: Meadowsweet contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties. These compounds can help protect the lining of the stomach and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.

Phenolic Acids: Meadowsweet has high levels of phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. These compounds can help alleviate pain and inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby reducing symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried meadowsweet flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the meadowsweet flowers and let them steep for 10 minutes.
Cover the teapot or cup to retain the heat and volatile oils.
After 10 minutes, remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Drink the meadowsweet tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day after meals to help with hyperacidity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Catnip tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that aid in soothing the gastrointestinal tract and reducing acid production. The volatile oils present in catnip, such as nepetalactone, act on the smooth muscle of the digestive system, promoting relaxation and alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity. Furthermore, catnip tea has carminative effects, which help in expelling gas from the stomach and easing the discomfort caused by acid reflux. Additionally, it is known to have calming and sedative properties that can help reduce stress-induced hyperacidity, making it an ideal natural remedy for those seeking relief from acid-related digestive issues. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating catnip tea or any other herbal remedy into your diet to manage hyperacidity, especially if you are taking medications or have underlying health conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the catnip plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated stomach lining.

remedy_constituents: Nepetalactone: This compound found in catnip has anti-gas properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as bloating and stomach discomfort.
Valepotriates: These compounds in catnip have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, which can help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the digestive tract caused by hyperacidity.
Nepetalic acid: This organic acid in catnip has a calming effect on the stomach and digestive system, which can help regulate acid production and reduce symptoms of hyperacidity.

remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed catnip leaves and flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to release their properties.
Discard the used catnip and pour the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for symptom relief.

remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling point (approximately 2-3 minutes in a microwave or 5-10 minutes on a stovetop).
